title: Add an issues (open/closed) panel to a dashboard

blocks.content: In this milestone, you create a new dashboard and add a panel that displays GitHub issues data, including the count of open and closed issues over time.
Navigate to **Dashboards** from the main menu.

Open the panel editor by doing one of the following:

- If you see an **Add visualization** button or placeholder, click it.
- If you see a **Configure visualization** button, click it.
- If you see a **+** icon in the top toolbar, click it and select **Visualization**.

Any of these options opens the panel editor.
Click the **data source** dropdown and select your GitHub data source from the list. If you have multiple data sources, look for the one named after your GitHub connection.
In the query builder, click the **Query Type** dropdown and choose **Issues**.
In the **Time Field** dropdown, select **Created At**.

blocks.reftarget: [data-testid='data-testid Panel editor option pane field input Title']
blocks.content: In the **Panel options** on the right, enter `GitHub Issues (Open/Closed) Overview` in the **Title** field.
Click **Save dashboard** to save the panel to your dashboard.

The panel now displays a Table visualization showing issue trends over time.

In the next milestone, you'll create a similar panel for pull request data.
